problem_statement: Knowledge base search is using prefix-only matching, so articles are only returned when the query appears at the start of the topic/title/content fields. Queries that match mid-field text are incorrectly excluded.
goal: Update knowledge base search so it performs contains/substring matching across article topic, title, and content, returning any article where the query appears anywhere in those fields.
acceptance_criteria: Searching for a term that appears in the middle of an article's topic, title, or content returns that article.
Existing prefix matches continue to work.
Search behavior is consistent across all indexed/searchable knowledge article fields.
constraints: Change should be localized to the backend knowledge search implementation.
Preserve existing API contract unless a broader change is required.
unknowns: Exact search backend implementation details in knowledge_service.py are not provided here.
Need to confirm whether search is implemented in Python filtering or in a database query.
repo_summary: Backend contains the main application code; likely knowledge article search is implemented in backend/app/services/knowledge_service.py with routes and models nearby.

targeted_symbols.reason: Issue explicitly points to this file and the search behavior is described as prefix-only matching; the fix likely lives in the search method or its query construction.
related_symbols: any query builder or repository methods used by search

localization_rationale: The issue explicitly names backend/app/services/knowledge_service.py (search) as the affected file.
The behavior described is a classic string matching bug in the search method: startswith/prefix logic needs to become substring/contains logic.
Other files (routes, models, schemas) are less likely to contain the matching logic itself; they may only pass parameters or shape responses.
